{{Quote|It's a metal-crystal phase shifter. MCPS for short.|Doxin reports to Tol Sivon|<ref name="Champions of the Force" />}}
'''Paldis Doxin''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] and engineer within the [[Magrody Institute of Programmable Intelligence/Legends|Magrody Institute]]. In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], he was involved in [[Hypermatter/Legends|hypermatter]] research at the [[Ministry of Science (Galactic Republic)/Legends|Ministry of Science]]. Chosen by [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]] to become a head scientist within the [[Maw Installation]], he led the team that developed the [[Metal-Crystal Phase Shifter]] used in the [[Battle of the Maw]].<ref name="Champions of the Force" />

==Biography==
{{Quote|Director, I've filed several reports over the past seven weeks. Haven't you read them?|Paldis Doxin to Tol Sivron|<ref name="Champions of the Force" />}}
Paldis Doxin was one of [[Bevel Lemelisk]]'s prize students, and when Lemelisk wandered from job to job, he brought Doxin with him. When Lemelisk was assigned to work on the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] project, Doxin was overjoyed to be involved as well, and happily transferred to Maw Installation to work on his mentor's team. Over several years, he contributed to the implementation of the project by developing the Death Star's laser focusing system. But when Lemelisk went with Tarkin to work on the actual production-model Death Star in the Outer Rim, Doxin found himself left behind.

He was killed along with most of the [[Scientist/Legends|scientists]] at [[Maw Cluster/Legends|the Maw]] when [[Tol Sivron]] accidentally flew the [[Death Star prototype]] down a [[Black hole/Legends|black hole]] during the Battle of the Maw in [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]].<ref name="Champions of the Force" />
